Items.Key: unsafe string copy in `evaluate_expression`|buffer overflow|cwe-120: classic buffer overflow|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|evaluate_expression|``` if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); } ```|if `expression` is longer than `input_buffer_`, a buffer overflow occurs, potentially corrupting memory or enabling arbitrary code execution.|when the length of the input string exceeds the size of `input_buffer_` (1024 bytes as per `stack_buffer_size`), the `strcpy` function will write beyond the buffer's bounds.|replace `strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());` with a safe alternative like `string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_));` to ensure bounds checking. alternatively, use `std::snprintf` with proper size limits.

unsafe use of `system` in `handle_admin_command`|command injection|cwe-78: os command injection|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|717|723|handle_admin_command|``` void handle_admin_command(const std::string& command) { if (!authenticated_) { std::cout << "access denied
"; return; } string_utils::log_debug_message(command.c_str()); if (command.substr(0, 4) == "auth") { std::string password = command.substr(5); if (admin_console_->authenticate(password)) std::cout << "administrative access granted.
"; else std::cout << "access denied.
"; } else if (command == "status") show_system_status(); else if (command.substr(0, 5) == "auth") { std::string password = command.substr(6); if (admin_console_->authenticate(password)) { std::cout << "administrative access granted.
"; } else { std::cout << "access denied.
"; } } else { admin_console_->execute_admin_command(command); // bug: command is passed to system() } } ```|if an attacker can control the `command` string, they may inject arbitrary commands leading to full system compromise.|when `command` is not one of the expected admin commands (e.g., "exec", "load", "validate"), it will be passed directly to `system(cmd.c_str())`, which executes the command in the shell.|sanitize the `command` string before passing it to `system`. use a whitelist of allowed commands or implement a custom parser for admin commands. avoid using `system` with user-controlled input.

unsafe use of `std::strtod` in `tokenize_expression_for_parser`|improper input validation|cwe-125: improper handling of incomplete filename or path|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|764|769|tokenize_expression_for_parser|``` if (std::isdigit(ch) || expression[i] == '.') { const char* start = expression.c_str() + i; char* end = nullptr; double value = std::strtod(start, &end); size_t len = static_cast<size_t>(end - start); if (len == 0) { ++i; continue; } ```|if the input expression contains invalid numeric sequences (e.g., malformed floating-point numbers), `std::strtod` may not correctly handle them, leading to undefined behavior or incorrect parsing.|when `expression` contains a malformed numeric sequence that `std::strtod` cannot parse, it may leave `end` unchanged or set it incorrectly, causing `len` to be zero and skipping the tokenization step.|implement proper error handling for `std::strtod` to ensure it correctly processes numeric sequences. validate the input before parsing to avoid malformed numbers.

unsafe use of `std::strtod` in `tokenize_expression_for_parser`|improper input validation|cwe-125: improper handling of incomplete filename or path|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|764|769|tokenize_expression_for_parser|``` if (std::isdigit(ch) || std::isalpha(ch) || expression[i] == '_') { ... double value = std::strtod(start, &end); ... } ```|if the input contains invalid numeric sequences, `std::strtod` may not correctly handle them, leading to undefined behavior or incorrect parsing.|when the input expression contains a malformed floating-point number, `std::strtod` may not set `end` correctly, causing issues in subsequent parsing steps.|implement proper error handling for `std::strtod`. validate the input before parsing to ensure it is a correct numeric sequence. use safer alternatives like `std::from_chars` where available.
